steps.title: Apply slope-intercept form
steps.content: A line with slope $m$ and y-intercept $b$ is $y = mx + b$. Given slope $-2$ and the point $(0, -19)$, the y-intercept is $-19$.

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: $f(x) = -19x$ — slope $-19$, wrong slope. Eliminated.
  • Choice B: $f(x) = -2x$ — y-intercept 0, not $-19$. Eliminated.
  • Choice C: $f(x) = -2x - 19$ — slope $-2$ and y-intercept $-19$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
  • Choice D: $f(x) = 2x + 19$ — slope $+2$, wrong sign. Eliminated.
